vehicle: SLS Block 1 (crew launch) + Starship HLS (lunar lander)
payload: Orion + 4-person crew; HLS pre-positioned at NRHO
credit: © NASA — Artemis program. Architecture: SLS launches Orion to Near-Rectilinear Halo Orbit (NRHO) where it docks with a pre-positioned Starship Human Landing System; 2 crew descend to surface, 2 remain in NRHO. Specific landing site TBD; 13 candidate regions in south polar zone.

launch.source: NASA Artemis 3 mission profile (planned; ICPS performs TLI for Orion). Crewed mass + service module + LAS jettisoned

cruise.source: NASA Artemis architecture documents (planned-mission target; outbound TCM + NRHO insertion correction)

arrival.source: NASA Artemis 3 mission plan; NRHO entry burn ~0.45 km/s vs Apollo's 0.89 km/s direct LLO insertion (NRHO is energetically favourable)

totals.source: NASA Artemis architecture (planned); SLS-Orion + HLS combined ledger
